outputLow.title: Result
outputLow.default: Based on your answers, no requirements from the AI Act apply.

outputHigh.title: Result
outputHigh.default: Based on your answers, your application is a high-risk AI system according to the AI Act. Your AI system must comply with the requirements for high-risk AI systems. The extent of the requirements for your organization depends on your organization's role for this application. Determine in consultation with relevant legal experts whether your organization is a user or provider.

outputHighSecA.title: Result
outputHighSecA.default: Based on your answers, your AI system is a high-risk AI system as a safety component of, or itself a product under, Annex I Section A harmonisation legislation that requires a third-party conformity assessment (Article 6(1) of the AI Act). The full set of high-risk obligations under the AI Act applies, including Articles 8 to 15, Article 16, Article 17, conformity assessment, registration, and post-market monitoring. Determine in consultation with relevant legal experts which specific obligations apply to your organisation's role.

outputHighSecB.title: Result
outputHighSecB.default: Based on your answers, your AI system is a high-risk AI system as a safety component of, or itself a product under, Annex I Section B sectoral legislation that requires a third-party conformity assessment (Article 6(1) of the AI Act). For Annex I Section B products only Article 6(1), Articles 102 to 109 (which integrate AI requirements into the relevant sectoral regulations) and Article 112 (Commission evaluation) of the AI Act apply directly; the detailed obligations of Articles 8 to 17 are integrated into the sectoral regulations governing your product.

outputForbidden.title: Result
outputForbidden.default: Based on your answers, your application (partially) meets the definition of a prohibited AI system according to the AI Act. Since this is the highest risk category, which means the AI system should not be used, we cannot and do not want to determine this based on a questionnaire. Engage legal expertise to determine in consultation which category this case falls into.

outputHighExcept.title: Result
outputHighExcept.default: Based on your answers, your application is a high-risk AI system according to the AI Act, but there may also be an exception. Determine in consultation with relevant legal experts whether an exception actually applies. If there is no exception, your AI system must comply with the requirements for high-risk AI systems.

outputForbiddenExcept.title: Result
outputForbiddenExcept.default: Based on your answers, your application (partially) meets the definition of a prohibited AI system according to the AI Act, but there may also be an exception. Determine in consultation with relevant legal experts whether an exception actually applies. If not, determine in consultation which category this case falls into.

III.1.ui:description: Biometric data are unique characteristics of a person such as images or video footage of faces, iris scans, fingerprints, voice sounds, handwriting, and other unique characteristics of bodies and/or behavior.
III.1.ui:alertDescription:
The key distinction of biometric data is: does the data measure a unique, stable, biological or behavioral trait of the body itself – or does it merely describe or record something associated with a person?

Examples of biometric data:
- Fingerprints, iris or retina patterns
- Facial and hand geometry, voice print, vein patterns, ear shape
- DNA
- Someone's unique way of typing (keystrokes) or walking (as captured by sensors or video analysis).

Examples of non-biometric data:
- A photo of someone
- Typed text or written signature
- Name or ID number
- Medical diagnosis
- Location or GPS data
- Passwords
- IP address or device fingerprints (tied to devices, not bodies)
- Age or birth date, height and weight.
III.1.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(1) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Yes: Biometric data is used
ui:id-summary.No: No biometric data is used

III.1.1.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(1) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:badges.label: Art. 5(1)(e)-(h) AI Act - Prohibited AI practices

ui:enumTooltips: Emotions include happiness, joy, sadness, anger, aggression, surprise, disgust, shyness, excitement, shame, contempt, satisfaction, and pleasure.

This means that people are grouped or assigned a label based on certain characteristics, such as how they look or behave. For example, if an application labels people as 'shoplifters' or makes an estimate of someone's gender based on video images.

Identification is about discovering who someone is from a group of people. For example, if you have a photo of a face, you can try to find out who that person is by comparing the photo with a database of faces. 'Remote' means that this identification takes place without the active involvement of the person being identified.

ui:id-summary.Creates or expands a facial recognition: The AI system creates or expands a facial recognition database
ui:id-summary.Recognizes or infers emotions or intenti: The AI system recognizes or infers emotions or intentions
The AI system is used for remote identification of individuals
The AI system uses biometric data for another purpose

5.e.2.ui:id-badge-label: Art. 5(1)(e) AI Act - Prohibited AI practices

ui:id-summary.Yes: Large-scale collection of facial images from the internet or security cameras (scraping) is involved
ui:id-summary.No: No large-scale scraping of facial images is involved

5.h.verify.ui:alertDescription:
Verification and authentication mean confirming an already-known identity: the person is present and willing, and the system checks them against a reference that belongs to them. If the person is unknown, passiveor not being matched against their own pre-stored reference, the system is not verifying or authenticating – it is trying to find out who someone is (identification) or infer something about them (categorisation).

Examples of verification and authentication:
- Unlocking a phone by matching your face to the face stored on the device
- Fingerprint reader giving access to building by matching your fingerprint to your enrolled profile
- A camera checks if your face matches the photo in your passport.

Examples not involving verification or authentication, but identification or categorization:
- A camera at a stadium entrance scans every face in the crowd to find a wanted suspect (identification)
- An airport uses facial recognition to spot known terrorists among passengers without their knowledge (identification)
- A shop uses cameras to detect the emotions or stress levels of customers (categorisation)
- An HR system analyses job applicants' facial expressions during video interviews (categorisation)
- A system checks whether someone walking past a camera is male or female based on their face (biometric categorisation)
- A police database is searched with CCTV footage to identify who committed a crime (identification).
5.h.verify.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(1) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Yes: The AI system is used only for verification or authentication of a known identity
ui:id-summary.No: The AI system is used beyond verification or authentication

III.1.2.ui:id-badge-label: Art. 5(1)(h) AI Act - Prohibited AI practices

ui:id-summary.No: The AI system is not used for law-enforcement purposes

III.2.4.ui:alertDescription:
A component with a safety function is intended to eliminate or reduce a risk. If the failure or malfunction of the AI system endangers the health and safety of people or property, choose yes. Examples of components with a safety function are:
- A system that automatically closes a lane for a tunnel during road congestion to prevent traffic jams in the tunnel
- Fire alarm systems
- Other detection or alarm functions that respond to over/under pressure, temperatureor voltage
- Collision avoidance systems in vehicles that automatically brake to prevent a collision
- Firewall or antivirus software.
III.2.4.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(2) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.No: The AI system does not fulfill a safety function in critical infrastructure

ui:id-summary.Placing targeted job advertisements: The AI system is used to place targeted job advertisements
ui:id-summary.Analyzing or filtering job applications: The AI system analyzes or filters job applications (e.g., CVs, cover letters, application videos or interviews)
ui:id-summary.Assessing candidates (e.g., aptitude tes: The AI system assesses candidates (e.g., aptitude tests, automatic assessment based on video)
ui:id-summary.Decisions regarding changed contractual: The AI system informs decisions on changed contractual terms, promotions or position changes
ui:id-summary.Decisions in the area of employee outflo: The AI system informs decisions on employee outflow or termination
ui:id-summary.Determining the distribution of tasks am: The AI system determines the distribution of tasks among employees
ui:id-summary.None of the above: The AI system is not used for any of the listed employment tasks

III.4.8.ui:description:
Think of administrative processes where hours, productivity, speed, breaks, performanceor behavior are processed.

We mean employees in the broadest sense, including employees but also freelancers and employees hired through a third party (temporary, secondment).
III.4.8.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(4) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Yes: The AI system is used in processes handling employee activity, behavior or performance data
ui:id-summary.No: The AI system does not process employee activity, behavior or performance data

III.4.9.ui:description: Think of monitoring productivity or speed or other performance.
III.4.9.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(4) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Yes: Employee behavior or performance is evaluated or monitored in this or related processes
ui:id-summary.No: Employee behavior or performance is not evaluated or monitored

III.5.decision.ui:description: Also choose 'yes' if the AI system is part of a preparatory step for these decisions, such as selection for control.
III.5.decision.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(5)(a) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Yes: The AI system assesses eligibility for public services, provisions or benefits
ui:id-summary.No: The AI system does not assess eligibility for public services, provisions or benefits

III.5.private.ui:description: Emergency services include police, fire brigade, and ambulance. Credit scoring also includes determining if someone can get a payment arrangement for taxes, fines or repaying a benefit.
III.5.private.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(5)(b)-(d) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Creditworthiness assessment or credit sc: The AI system is used for creditworthiness assessment or credit scoring of individuals
ui:id-summary.Risk assessment or pricing for life or h: The AI system is used for risk assessment or pricing for life or health insurance
ui:id-summary.Evaluating emergency calls, deploying or: The AI system evaluates emergency calls, deploys or prioritizes emergency services, or triages urgent medical care
ui:id-summary.None of the above: The AI system is not used for any of the listed financial or emergency purposes

III.6-5.d.ui:description: Also think of (administrative) offenses and fraud risk scores, where an estimate is made of whether someone might commit a criminal offense and an estimate of the likelihood of recidivism.

ui:badges.label: Art. 5 AI Act - Prohibited AI practices

III.6-5.d.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III.6-5.d AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Yes: The AI system assesses the risk that someone will (re)commit a criminal offense
ui:id-summary.No: The AI system does not assess the risk of (re)committing a criminal offense

III.6.uses.ui:description: Also choose an option when the AI system plays a part in the process.
III.6.uses.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(6) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Assess or estimate that someone will bec: The AI system assesses or estimates that someone will become a victim of criminal offenses
ui:id-summary.Act as a polygraph (lie detector) or sim: The AI system acts as a polygraph (lie detector) or similar instrument
ui:id-summary.Assess the reliability of evidence durin: The AI system assesses the reliability of evidence during investigation or prosecution of criminal offenses
ui:id-summary.Automatically evaluate or predict charac: The AI system automatically evaluates or predicts characteristics, behavior or personal aspects in the context of crime detection, investigation or prosecution
The AI system automatically evaluates or predicts characteristics, behavior or personal aspects in the context of crime detection, investigation or prosecution
ui:id-summary.Assess personality traits, characteristi: The AI system assesses personality traits, characteristics or previous criminal behavior of individuals or groups
ui:id-summary.None of the above: The AI system is not used for any of the listed law-enforcement tasks

III.7.uses.ui:description: Also choose 'yes' when the AI system is used in assessing the reliability of evidence for applications or complaints.
ui:enumTooltips: Think of a safety or health risk that a migrant or asylum seeker brings, such as tuberculosis screening.
III.7.uses.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(7) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Act as a polygraph (lie detector) or sim: The AI system acts as a polygraph (lie detector) or similar instrument
ui:id-summary.Assess risks for a person entering the t: The AI system assesses risks for a person entering the territory of a Member State of the European Union
ui:id-summary.Process asylum, visaor residence permit: The AI system processes asylum, visa or residence permit applications, or handles eligibility-related complaints
ui:id-summary.None of the above: The AI system is not used for any of the listed migration or border-control tasks

III.7.4.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(7) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Yes: The AI system is used for detection, recognition or identification of individuals in migration or border control
ui:id-summary.No: The AI system is not used for detection, recognition or identification of individuals

III.7.5.ui:description: Choose 'no' when the AI system is also used for identification that is broader than just checking a passport or other travel document.
III.7.5.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(7) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Yes: The AI system is used solely to verify a passport or other travel document
ui:id-summary.No: The AI system is used for identification beyond passport verification

III.8.2.ui:description: Think of anonymizing or pseudonymizing judicial decisions, documents or data, communication between staff members, and administrative tasks.
III.8.2.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(8)(a) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Yes: The AI system is used only for administrative activities that do not affect the judiciary in individual cases
ui:id-summary.No: The AI system is used beyond supporting administrative activities in the judiciary

III.8.elections.ui:alertDescription: This does not include AI systems to the output of which natural persons are not directly exposed, such as tools used to organise, optimise or structure political campaigns from an administrative or logistical point of view.
III.8.elections.ui:id-badge-label: Annex III(8)(b) AI Act - High-risk AI systems referred to in Article 6(2)

ui:id-summary.Yes: The AI system is used in elections or referenda in a way that can influence the outcome or voting behavior of people
ui:id-summary.No: The AI system is not used in a way that can influence elections or voting behavior

5.a.ui:description: Subliminal techniques include showing images so briefly that they cannot be consciously perceived. Manipulation without awareness includes AI chatbots that give advice influencing decisions, applications that stimulate addictionor systems that prevent users from filing a complaint while they intended to.
5.a.ui:id-badge-label: Art. 5(1)(a) AI Act - Prohibited AI practices

ui:id-summary.Yes: The AI system uses subliminal or manipulative techniques that operate without users' awareness
ui:id-summary.No: The AI system does not use subliminal or manipulative techniques

5.a.4.ui:description: Think of an AI health chatbot intended to give tips on a healthy lifestyle and tailored advice for psychological and physical exercises. However, if the chatbot encourages unhealthy habits or dangerous activities (e.g., excessive exercise without rest or drinking water) that they would not have done otherwise, it has negative consequences for the user.
5.a.4.ui:id-badge-label: Art. 5.a AI Act - Prohibited AI practices

ui:id-summary.Yes: The AI system can potentially have negative consequences for the user or other people
ui:id-summary.No: The AI system has no potential negative consequences for the user or other people

5.c.ui:alertDescription:
Examples of social behavior are:
- Driving behavior
- Communication style
- Breaking (un)written rules

Examples of personal characteristics are:
- Age
- Height
- Gender
- Economic situation
- Movement patterns
- Interests
5.c.ui:id-badge-label: Art. 5(1)(c) AI Act - Prohibited AI practices

ui:id-summary.Yes: The AI system evaluates, scores or classifies individuals in a way that could lead to unfavorable treatment
ui:id-summary.No: The AI system does not evaluate, score or classify individuals in a way that could lead to unfavorable treatment

5.c.treat.ui:description:
Unfavorable treatment in an unrelated context is, for example, a fraud risk score that leads to an investigation, where the fraud risk score is based on characteristics without clear relevance to the assessment of fraud, such as having a partner with a certain nationality or behavior on social platforms.

Disproportionate treatment is, for example, someone getting a higher fraud risk score because municipal taxes were once paid late or a student not being admitted to further education because they were once caught cheating.
5.c.treat.ui:id-badge-label: Art. 5(1)(c) AI Act - Prohibited AI practices

ui:id-summary.Yes: The unfavorable treatment occurs in an unrelated context or is disproportionate to the behavior or characteristic it is based on
ui:id-summary.No: The unfavorable treatment is neither in an unrelated context nor disproportionate

exceptions.ui:description: When the AI system is tested or used in operational processes or regular business operations, choose 'None of the above'.
ui:enumTooltips: This includes AI systems developed by students as part of their studies or by scientists affiliated with a university. In that case, the research must follow recognized ethical and professional standards for scientific research. For example, all participants must be informed that they are part of scientific research.

National security purposes mean preventing and punishing activities that can seriously destabilize the constitutional, political, economicor social structures of a country. Think of direct threats to society, the populationor the state itself, such as terrorist activities.

Note: When the AI system is also used for other activities or purposes, such as regular prevention, detection, and investigation of criminal offenses, choose 'None of the above'.

ui:id-summary.Scientific research and scientific devel: An exception applies: the AI system is used for scientific research and development
An exception applies: the AI system is used for defense or military purposes
An exception applies: the AI system is used for national security purposes

6.1.ui:description: Think of automatically evaluating professional performance, economic situation, health, personal preferences, interests, reliability, behavior, locationor movements of a person to analyze or predict.
6.1.ui:id-badge-label: Art. 6(3) AI Act - Classification rules for high-risk AI systems

ui:id-summary.Yes: Profiling is used — the AI system automatically evaluates or predicts characteristics, behavior or personal aspects
ui:id-summary.No: Profiling is not used

ui:enumTooltips: For example: converting unstructured data into structured data, an AI system that classifies incoming documents into categoriesor an AI system used to detect duplicates within a large number of applications.
For example: an AI system that improves the language of previously drafted documents, for example, regarding professional tone or academic styleor by tailoring a text to a specific brand message.
For example: an AI system that, after a set of decisions has been made, analyzes patterns in decision-making so that human decision-makers can learn from them.

For example: smart solutions for file management, including functions such as indexing, searching, text and speech processingor linking data to other data sourcesor AI systems used for translating source documents.
6.2.ui:id-badge-label: Art. 6(3) AI Act - Classification rules for high-risk AI systems

ui:id-summary.Yes, the AI system is used for a narrow: The AI system is used only for a narrow procedural task
ui:id-summary.Yes, the AI system is used to improve th: The AI system is used only to improve the result of a previously human-performed activity
ui:id-summary.Yes, the AI system is used to detect dec: The AI system is used only to detect decision-making patterns or deviations from previous patterns
ui:id-summary.Yes, the AI system is used in a preparat: The AI system is used only in a preparatory step, without influencing the outcome
ui:id-summary.No: The AI system is not limited to narrow procedural or preparatory tasks
